key_insight: A pretrained image-editing model can act as a general numerical solver when physical fields and parameters are encoded as images and lightweight adapters.
engineering_relevance: This suggests a single vision-based framework could prototype or approximate a wide range of PDE and mechanics problems without task-specific solver architectures.
method: Render inputs and outputs as images for each physics problem.
Inject scalar parameters through lightweight adapters.
Train on supervision from established numerical and analytic solvers across heterogeneous tasks.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports successful modeling of elliptic, diffusion, fluid, elasticity, travel-time, fracture, and transport problems, including unstable and shock-like behavior.
watch_item: Image and latent representations make it harder to control numerical range and enforce governing equations or invariants, and chaotic long-horizon dynamics may fail due to representation error.
paper_title: Image Editing Models are Numerical Solvers
